reason: Both land on the same core recommendation (take it, contingent on a clear use-of-funds plan) and both get the key math right: ~16.7% dilution, and correct compounding (A: ~$75k MRR in 12mo at 8%/mo; B: ~$88k in 14mo, both check out). Both hit the acceleration-vs-survival framing and the use-of-funds decision test. B goes further on the "terms beyond headline valuation" key point with concrete, actionable asks (1x non-participating pref, cap board seat, standard pro-rata), which is genuinely useful new content, not padding. B also adds a decision-relevant fact A lacks: the next round likely needs $80-100k MRR for equal or better terms, quantifying the cost of waiting. A's equivalent move (negotiate price, shop the term sheet) is a fair but thinner treatment of the terms dimension. Neither has a disqualifying factual error. B is marginally more useful due to the concrete negotiation guidance and added market-timing fact, delivered compactly rather than as padding.
